titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paola Agosti Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 13, 2025 at 23:06

did.unittitle: Paola Agosti photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from S. T. di Matteo di Castro on the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2016.
Purchased from S. T. di Matteo di Castro on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.
Purchased from S. T. di Matteo di Castro on the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Feminist Movement in Italy, 1974-2016. II. Gay Rights Movement in Italy, 1976-2017. III. Women in Factories, 1983-2017. IV. Piedmont, 1977-2017. V. South America, 1970-2020. VI. Cuba, 1995-2020. VII. United States, 1972-2020. VIII. Collection Inventories, undated.

bioghist.head: Paola Agosti (1947-)
bioghist.p: Paola Agosti is an Italian photographer. Born in Turin, Italy in 1947, she briefly attended the Albertina Academy of Fine Arts before moving to Rome in 1968, where she began working as a freelance photographer in 1969. Throughout her career, she has documented social movements in Italy and abroad, particularly the Italian feminist movement of the 1970s. Her other projects depict farmers in rural Piedmont and trace emigration from the region to Argentina. Her work also includes portraits of worldwide political leaders and cultural figures. Agosti has published over twenty books of photography, beginning with Riprendiamoci la vita: Immagini del movimento delle donne in 1976. Her work has been exhibited throughout Italy, Europe, and the Americas, including at the Venice Biennial International Art Exhibition.
Biographical note above is adapted from: Agosti, Paola. "Vita." Translated by Kevin Repp. Accessed 23 August 2021. http://www.paolaagosti.com/page.php?sez=vita.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paola Agosti Photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

This finding aid contains original creator-supplied titles. Devised titles are enclosed in brackets, and all other folder titles are original. Original folder titles may include racist, outdated, or harmful descriptive language. Original folder titles have been maintained to preserve the original context of how the creator labeled the files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of 667 black-and-white and color photographic prints by Italian photographer Paola Agosti documenting social change, political movements, and daily life in Italy, South America, Cuba, and the United States.

Agosti's photographs of Italy depict the feminist movement and women's rights organizations Movimento di Liberazione della Donna and Unione Donne Italiane in the 1970s and 1980s, the gay rights collective Fronte Unitario Omosessuale Rivoluzionario Italiano in 1976, women factory workers in Northern Italy in 1983, and the decline of traditional peasant society in rural Piedmont during the 1970s and 1980s. The collection also includes photographs of Piedmontese immigrants in Argentina in the late 1980s and 1990s, and images documenting social movements in Chile after the 1970 election, such as protests by the Mapuche people. There is a small selection of images taken in Colombia, Paraguay, Peru, and Venezuela. Other photographs show women in Cuba in 1995, cityscapes across the United States in the 1970s and 1980s, Black residents of Roxbury, Boston, and political demonstrations at the 1972 Democratic National Convention. Notable subjects in the collection include Italian gay rights activist Mario Mieli, Italian writer Nuto Revelli, Cuban politicians Fidel Castro and Osvaldo Dorticós Torrado, Chilean President Salvador Allende, and American poet Allen Ginsberg.

All photographs were assigned a catalog code number from Y1 to Y667, which are labeled on the verso of prints and listed in the collection inventories. Photographs are signed by Agosti and inscribed with manuscript captions in Italian. While the original images were taken from 1968 to 1995, the collection contains both vintage prints and artists' proofs printed by Agosti from 2016 to 2020. Materials are dated 1970 to 2020.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paola Agosti Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Democratic National Convention (1972 : Miami Beach, Fla.)

did.unittitle: Feminist Movement in Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 194 black-and-white photographic prints documenting the feminist movement in Italy from 1973 to 1985. Photographs depict demonstrations, protests, marches, and other public events in Rome, Florence, and Naples, particularly on the issues of abortion and violence against women. Photographs also document meetings and conventions by organizations such as Movimento di Liberazione della Donna and Unione Donne Italiane.
The original images were taken from 1973 to 1985. Photographic materials consist of vintage prints from 1974 to 1985 and artists' proofs printed in 2016.

did.unittitle: Gay Rights Movement in Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 31 black-and-white photographic prints of Fronte Unitario Omosessuale Rivoluzionario Italiano (FUORI!), Italy's first gay rights organization, during its fifth national congress in Rome in 1976. Images depict FUORI! co-founder Mario Mieli and other activists in attendance during meetings and public gatherings.
Photographs consist of vintage prints from 1976 and artists' proofs printed in 2016 and 2017.

did.unittitle: Women in Factories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 33 black-and-white photographic prints of women factory workers in Northern Italy in 1983. Images document scenes of the first female workers in the Alfa Romeo and Fiat automobile factories, computer and electronic factories, appliance factories, and spinning mills in Milan, Turin, and Biella.
Photographs consist of vintage prints from 1983 and artists' proofs printed in 2017.

did.unittitle: Piedmont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 208 black-and-white and color prints documenting peasant society in the province of Cuneo in the Piedmont region of Northern Italy from 1977 to 1985. Agosti was hired by Nuto Revelli when he was doing research for his oral history project on the region, Il mondo dei vinti (1977). Her photographs, including a portrait of Revelli at home, were collected in Immagine del "mondo dei vinti" (1979). She returned to Piedmont in the mid-1980s to photograph the same families. Her images capture the effects of post-war industrialization, modernization, migration, depopulation, and poverty on Piedmontese peasant farmers.

Photographs depict traditional life, work, and customs in rural villages and farms throughout the Cuneo province, including alpine valleys such as Grana Valley, Maira Valley, and Stura di Demonte Valley, and the Langhe wine-growing area. There are also images of ex-voto paintings in the area's churches and sanctuaries.
Photographs consist of vintage prints from 1977 to 1985 and artists' proofs printed in 2016 and 2017.

did.unittitle: South America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 126 black-and-white photographic prints of Argentina, Chile, Colombia, Paraguay, Peru, and Venezuela. The majority of images document Italian emigration from Piedmont and settlement in Argentina, dated 1977 to 1991. Subjects depicted include farmers, workers, families, and social life in urban and rural communities, in locations in Argentina such as Buenos Aires, Las Varillas, Rosario, and Freyre.

Other photographs document life in Chile following the election of president Salvador Allende in 1970, including scenes of workers, youth brigades, and Mapuche people protesting for land reform in Santiago and Temuco, as well as portraits of Allende.
Photographs consist of vintage prints from 1970 to 1991 and artists' proofs printed in 2020.

Cuba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 44 black-and-white photographic prints of Agosti's trips to Cuba in 1968 and 1995. The first three photographs were taken in 1968, while Agosti was a volunteer at the international student work camp Campamento Cinco de Mayo, and feature Fidel Castro and President Osvaldo Dorticós Torrado at a rally.

The remaining 41 photographs were taken in 1995 and focus on daily life for Cuban woman and girls at work, at home, and in public spaces. Images depict female cigar factory workers, soldiers, hairdressers, and other workers, as well as women with their families and friends. Locations primarily include Havana and Santiago.
Photographs consist of vintage prints from 1995 and artists' proofs printed in 2020.

did.unittitle: Y575 Santa Clara, Cuba, July 26, 1968. Fidel Castro and Osvaldo Dorticós Torrado attending celebrations for the anniversary of the Cuban revolution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

United States

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 31 black-and-white photographic prints from Agosti's various trips to the United States. Photographs include cityscapes in New York City, Las Vegas, and Washington D.C., and portraits of Black residents of Roxbury, Boston. Other images depict political demonstrations outside the 1972 Democratic National Convention in Miami Beach, such as a rally attended by Allen Ginsberg.
Photographs consist of original prints from 1972 to 1989 and artists' proofs printed in 2020.

did.unittitle: Y637 Miami Beach, USA, July 1972. Feminist performance during the Democratic National Convention

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image taken in 1972 and printed in 2020.

did.unittitle: Collection Inventories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three inventories prepared for the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library contain detailed information for all 667 photographs. Each entry lists a catalog number, title in Italian, English-translated title, description on the verso of photos, shooting medium, print medium, date of print, and dimensions in centimeters and inches.
Photographs Y1 through Y466 are described in two print catalogs filed in the collection. Photographs Y467 through Y667 are described in a digital spreadsheet.

did.unittitle: Catalog, "Paola Agosti photographs: Feminist and Gay movements in Italy 1973-1985"
Catalog, "Paola Agosti photographs: Gay rights movement, Women in factories, The world of losers"
Catalog, "Paola Agosti photographs: Americas"
